<p><strong> Instructions </strong></p>
<p><strong>Option 1: Manual User Invitation</strong></p>

<p>Use this method to manually invite users directly from the DevRev application instead of uploading a CSV.</p>

<p><strong>Steps</strong></p>

<ol>
  <li>Log in to DevRev and go to Settings. Navigate to User Management > Users.</li>
  <li>Click + Users in the top-right corner. This will open the user invitation flow.</li>
  <li>Enter the user’s work email address. Assign the user to the appropriate group (for example, Platform Users) and select the relevant license plan (for example, Support-Pro).</li>
  <li>Click Send Invite.</li>
</ol>

<p>The user will receive an email invitation with a link to accept and complete onboarding.</p>

<p><strong>Notes</strong></p>

<ul>
  <li>Ensure the correct group and license are selected to enable access to the intended learning path.</li>
  <li>Invitations are sent immediately upon submission.</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>Option 2: CSV Upload Invitation</strong></p>

<p>This file is used to invite users to DevRevU and automatically enroll them into the appropriate learning paths. The information provided determines how users are added to the platform, which enablement journey they are assigned to, and how their learning progress is tracked.</p>

<p><strong>Steps</strong></p>

<ol>
  <li>Create a spreadsheet with the following headers: Email Address | First Name | Last Name | Role.</li>
  <li>Add one user per row with accurate details.</li>
  <li>Save the file in CSV format.</li>
  <li>Upload the file to trigger user invitations.</li>
</ol>

<p><strong>Role Mapping</strong></p>

<p>Selecting the correct role ensures users are enrolled in the most relevant learning path.</p>

<ul>
  <li>Support → Support Enablement Path</li>
  <li>Build → Build Enablement Path</li>
  <li>Agentic → Agentic / AI Enablement Path</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>Note</strong></p>

<ul>
  <li>Please use official work email addresses to ensure proper access and tracking.</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>— Team DevRevU</strong></p>
